findings.title: Potential PII Handling Issues
findings.description: The transition to a consumption-based pricing model may lead to the collection of more PII than necessary, violating the data-minimal principle.
assumptions: The new pricing model will require more detailed usage data from customers.

evidence.excerpt: Migrate from flat monthly pricing to a consumption-based pricing model where customers pay per metric ingested.

recommendations: Implement strict data minimization practices to ensure only necessary PII is collected.

metadata.mitigation: Implement strict data minimization practices to ensure only necessary PII is collected.

findings.title: Lack of Consent Mechanism for New Data Collection
findings.description: The new pricing model may require additional customer data collection without a clear consent mechanism, violating privacy policies.
assumptions: Customers may not be informed about the new data collection practices.

evidence.excerpt: Migrate from flat monthly pricing to a consumption-based pricing model.

recommendations: Establish a clear consent mechanism for any new data collection related to the pricing model.

metadata.mitigation: Establish a clear consent mechanism for any new data collection related to the pricing model.

findings.title: Retention Policy Violations
findings.description: The new pricing model may lead to retention of customer data beyond the 90-day limit if not properly managed.
assumptions: Data retention policies may not be enforced during the transition.

evidence.excerpt: Migrate from flat monthly pricing to a consumption-based pricing model.

recommendations: Ensure that data retention policies are strictly enforced during the transition.

metadata.mitigation: Ensure that data retention policies are strictly enforced during the transition.

findings.title: Authentication Weaknesses in New Billing System
findings.description: The new billing engine may introduce authentication weaknesses if not properly secured, leading to unauthorized access to sensitive customer data.
assumptions: The new billing system will have the same authentication mechanisms as the previous system.

evidence.excerpt: Migrate from flat monthly pricing to a consumption-based pricing model.

recommendations: Implement strong authentication mechanisms for the new billing system.

metadata.mitigation: Implement strong authentication mechanisms for the new billing system.

findings.title: Data Exposure Risks During Pricing Transition
findings.description: The transition to a consumption-based pricing model may expose customer data if proper security measures are not implemented.
assumptions: Existing security measures may not be sufficient for the new model.

evidence.excerpt: Create a model that identifies which customer segments will see cost increases.

recommendations: Conduct a security review to identify and mitigate data exposure risks.

metadata.mitigation: Conduct a security review to identify and mitigate data exposure risks.

findings.title: Compliance Risks with New Pricing Model
findings.description: The new pricing model may not comply with GDPR and CCPA regulations if customer data is not handled properly.
assumptions: The new pricing model will require additional customer data.

evidence.excerpt: Develop a grandfathering system that tracks customer cohorts.

recommendations: Conduct a legal review to ensure compliance with GDPR and CCPA.

metadata.mitigation: Conduct a legal review to ensure compliance with GDPR and CCPA.

findings.title: Accessibility Compliance for New Features
findings.description: New pricing features may not comply with WCAG standards, potentially excluding users with disabilities.
assumptions: Accessibility considerations may be overlooked during development.

evidence.excerpt: Develop an in-app pricing calculator that estimates monthly costs.

recommendations: Ensure all new features comply with WCAG standards.

metadata.mitigation: Ensure all new features comply with WCAG standards.
metadata.policy_rule: Accessibility is a requirement, not an afterthought

summary: Identified 7 risks (1 accessibility, 1 compliance, 3 privacy, 2 security). 6 flagged as blockers. Risk gate: FAILED (7 blockers, 3 warnings).
